what: Introduction: which covariates belong in a propensity score model?
title: Getting Started with oalasso
text: propensity score (PS) model need — get — every covariate predicts treatment. matters treatment-effect estimate different set: confounders (covariates related treatment outcome) , efficiency, pure outcome predictors. Two kinds covariates kept PS model: Instruments — covariates predict treatment unrelated outcome except treatment. Including increases variance effect estimate without reducing bias (Brookhart et al. 2006), , confounding remains unmeasured, amplifies bias unmeasured confounding contributes (“Z-bias”; Myers et al. 2011). Strong instruments also push propensity scores toward 0 1, creating near-positivity violations unstable weights. Noise variables — covariates related neither treatment outcome, add variance. outcome-adaptive lasso (OAL) Shortreed & Ertefaie (2017) automates selection. fits logistic PS model adaptively weighted lasso penalty, covariate’s penalty weight |β̂j|−γ|\hat\beta_j|^{-\gamma} β̂j\hat\beta_j covariate’s coefficient linear regression outcome treatment covariates. Covariates outcome association get exploding penalty forced PS model; confounders outcome predictors survive. penalty strength λn\lambda_n tuned weighted absolute mean difference (wAMD), balance criterion weights covariate’s post-weighting imbalance outcome-coefficient magnitude — tuning, like penalty, outcome-oriented. oalasso implements OAL elastic-net generalization GOAL (Baldé, Yang & Lefebvre 2023; method = "goal"), top glmnet exact penalty-scale correction published objectives tuning grids reproduced (see vignette("method-details", package = "oalasso")). Like companion package psAve, deliverable deliberately modest: fit$ps plain numeric vector propensity scores, ready MatchIt::matchit() distance measure, WeightIt::weightit() propensity score, psAve::psave() appended candidate.

what: A first look: instruments are excluded, confounders are kept
title: Getting Started with oalasso
text: small simulated example spirit Shortreed & Ertefaie’s first scenario makes selection behavior visible. x1 x2 confounders, x3 x4 predict outcome, x5 x6 predict treatment (instruments): seed set data simulation : oal() fully deterministic (seed argument nothing stochastic happens inside).
code: library(oalasso)  set.seed(20260702) n <- 500 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 6), n, 6, dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("x", 1:6))) A <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(X %*% c(1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1))) Y <- as.numeric(2 * A + X %*% c(0.6, 0.6, 0.6, 0.6, 0, 0) + rnorm(n)) sim <- data.frame(A, Y, X)  fit.sim <- oal(A ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + x4 + x5 + x6, data = sim, outcome = ~ Y) fit.sim #> OAL (Shortreed & Ertefaie 2017, doi:10.1111/biom.12679) #> An oal object (outcome-adaptive lasso propensity score) #>  - estimand: ATE;  refit: FALSE (penalized fit) #>  - sample:   500 units (248 treated, 252 control); 6 covariate column(s) #>  - selected: delta = -10 (lambda_n = 1.02e-27), gamma = 26 #>  - wAMD at the selection: 0.122 #>  #> Retained (outcome-related): #>   x1  (|b| = 0.572) #>   x2  (|b| = 0.647) #>   x3  (|b| = 0.600) #>   x4  (|b| = 0.618) #> Excluded (outcome-unrelated): #>   x5  (|b| = 0.00339) #>   x6  (|b| = 0.06668) #>  #> Excluded covariates showed no outcome association; excluding instruments and noise variables from a propensity score model improves precision and avoids bias amplification (Brookhart et al. 2006; Myers et al. 2011). #>  #> Next: #>   MatchIt::matchit(A ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + x4 + x5 + x6, data = sim, distance = x$ps) #>     or: oal_match(x) #>   WeightIt::weightit(A ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + x4 + x5 + x6, data = sim, ps = x$ps, estimand = "ATE") #>     or: oal_weight(x) #>   ($ps is numeric, named by rownames(sim), strictly inside (0, 1); #>    it also satisfies psAve::psave(ps.append = cbind(oal = x$ps)).)

what: How the tuning works: the wAMD criterion
title: Getting Started with oalasso
text: candidate penalty λn=nδ\lambda_n = n^\delta grid (lambda supplies exponents δ\delta; default Shortreed & Ertefaie’s grid), oal() fits penalized PS model, forms IPW weights wiw_i clipped scores, computes weighted absolute mean difference wAMD(λn)=∑j=1p|β̂j||∑iwiXijAi∑iwiAi−∑iwiXij(1−Ai)∑iwi(1−Ai)|, \mathrm{wAMD}(\lambda_n) \;=\; \sum_{j=1}^{p} |\hat\beta_j| \, \left| \frac{\sum_i w_i X_{ij} A_i}{\sum_i w_i A_i}      - \frac{\sum_i w_i X_{ij} (1 - A_i)}{\sum_i w_i (1 - A_i)} \right|, summed covariate columns (standardized scale), including model excluded. grid point minimizing wAMD selected. covariate’s imbalance weighted outcome-coefficient magnitude |β̂j||\hat\beta_j|, residual imbalance instrument (β̂j≈0\hat\beta_j \approx 0) costs almost nothing — criterion deliberately reward balancing covariates cause confounding. δ\delta paired penalty exponent γ=2(2−δ+1)\gamma = 2(2 - \delta + 1) pair satisfies method’s theoretical rate conditions; vignette("method-details", package = "oalasso") gives formulas, provenance every default, exact glmnet correction makes published objective reproducible. criterion path stored plottable:  oal_wamd() — exported single source truth criterion — scores candidate propensity score formula, convenient comparing OAL score external one.

what: Two packages, two kinds of propensity score model uncertainty
title: Using oalasso with psAve
text: oalasso psAve companion packages address different sources uncertainty propensity score (PS) analysis: covariates? outcome-adaptive lasso (Shortreed & Ertefaie 2017) selects covariates belong PS model — confounders outcome predictors — excludes instruments noise variables, whose inclusion inflates variance can amplify unmeasured-confounding bias (Brookhart et al. 2006; Myers et al. 2011). model form? psAve::psave() (Kabata, Stuart & Shintani 2024) averages candidate PS models different functional forms (logistic regression, CART, random forest, gradient boosting, …), convex mixing weights selected balance prognostic score. two compose cleanly packages speak minimal contract: propensity score plain numeric vector length nn, named rownames(data), strictly inside (0, 1). psave() accepts vector extra candidate via ps.append argument, OAL score can compete — averaged — flexible learners: Contract compliance construction: oal() errors missing data rather dropping rows (reordering shrinking can misalign rows), default clip = c(0.01, 0.99) equals psave()’s default clip (psave’s re-clipping appended column -op), fit$ps carries rownames data, psave() checks. Passing bare vector (ps.append = fit$ps) also works; candidate labeled "append" psave’s output. Wrapping one-column matrix, cbind(oal = fit$ps), keeps rownames psave’s alignment check gives candidate readable label.
code: fit <- oal(treat ~ x1 + x2 + ..., data = dat, outcome = ~ y, estimand = "ATT") ma  <- psAve::psave(treat ~ x1 + x2 + ..., data = dat, outcome = ~ y,                     ps.append = cbind(oal = fit$ps))

title: oalasso: Outcome-Adaptive Lasso Propensity Scores
text: Outcome-adaptive lasso propensity scores — published methods, exactly. oalasso implements outcome-adaptive lasso (OAL) Shortreed & Ertefaie (2017) generalized outcome-adaptive lasso (GOAL) Baldé, Yang & Lefebvre (2023) propensity score estimation. OAL fits logistic propensity score model adaptively weighted lasso penalty whose weights come outcome regression: covariates unrelated outcome — instruments noise variables — receive exploding penalty excluded, confounders outcome predictors retained. Excluding instruments propensity score model improves precision avoids bias amplification unmeasured confounding (Brookhart et al. 2006; Myers et al. 2011). Tuning weighted absolute mean difference (wAMD) balance criterion, method = "goal" adds Baldé’s elastic-net ridge term correlated covariates fragile positivity. Fidelity package’s identity. solver glmnet exact, KKT-verified penalty-scale correction, published objectives tuning grids (Shortreed & Ertefaie’s λn=nδ\lambda_n = n^\delta grid paired γ\gamma) reproduced rather silently reinterpreted glmnet’s internal penalty-factor rescaling. Every fit prints provenance line stating exactly published method implements — flags anything goes beyond validated territory. pipeline fully deterministic (seed needed). oalasso second package suite psAve, companion MatchIt/WeightIt/cobalt ecosystem. deliverable plain numeric vector propensity scores: fit$ps drops MatchIt::matchit() distance measure, WeightIt::weightit() propensity score, psAve::psave() appended candidate; thin oal_match() / oal_weight() wrappers without retyping formula, cobalt::bal.tab() works fitted object directly. Effect estimation stays belongs, MatchIt/WeightIt/survey/marginaleffects.

Zeros allowed mean hard drop: \(|0|^{-\gamma} = \infty\) glmnet natively converts infinite penalty factor exclusion. epsilon cap ever applied. title: Outcome-adaptive lasso propensity scores — oal
text: OAL solves, TOTAL log-likelihood scale, $$\hat\alpha = \arg\min_\alpha\; -\ell(\alpha; , X_s)   + \lambda_n \sum_j |b_j|^{-\gamma} |\alpha_j|,$$ \(\ell\) binomial log-likelihood treatment standardized covariates \(b_j\) outcome-model coefficients. glmnet minimizes per-observation loss internally rescales penalty factors sum number variables, grid point evaluated exactly $$s = \overline{\mathrm{pen}} \cdot \lambda_n / n, \qquad   \mathrm{pen}_j = |b_j|^{-\gamma},$$ via coef(fit, s = s, exact = TRUE, x = , y = , penalty.factor = ), re-solves penalty (KKT-verified recipe Shortreed/Ertefaie rejoinder code). outcome.coef contains zeros, \(\mathrm{pen}_j = \infty\): glmnet converts exclusions internally resets factor 1 sum--nvars rescale, constant generalizes \(s = (\sum_{j: \mathrm{finite}} \mathrm{pen}_j + \#\{\mathrm{pen}_j = \infty\})/p \cdot \lambda_n / n\) – identical \(\overline{\mathrm{pen}}\,\lambda_n/n\) factors finite. correction always ; makes \(n^\delta\) grid carry published meaning.

Per-subset scaling structurally impossible. Coefficients back-transformed output: \(\alpha_j = \alpha^{std}_j / s_j\) \(\alpha_0 = \alpha^{std}_0 - \sum_j \alpha^{std}_j c_j / s_j\), centers \(c\) scales \(s\) stored info.

title: Outcome-adaptive lasso propensity scores — oal
text: lambda supplies exponents \(\delta\) \(\lambda_n = n^{\delta}\), n = nrow(model.matrix) always. gamma = NULL \(\delta\) paired \(\gamma = 2(\mathrm{gcf} - \delta + 1)\) (default \(\mathrm{gcf} = 2\)), formula derivable \(\lambda_n\, n^{\gamma/2 - 1} = n^{\mathrm{gcf}}\); \(\lambda \times \gamma\) cross-products paired mode. scalar gamma crossed \(\delta\) (Schnitzer's \(\gamma = 2.5\) reachable way; convert raw \(\lambda\) grid via \(\delta = \log \lambda_n / \log n\)).

title: Outcome-adaptive lasso propensity scores — oal
text: \(\lambda_2 > 0\) elastic-net term solved Zou-Hastie augmentation: \(X_{aug} = \mathrm{rbind}(X_s, \sqrt{\lambda_2} I_p)\) \(p\) pseudo-responses 0, followed rescaling coefficients INCLUDING intercept \((1 + \lambda_2)\) (Balde 2025 supplement: \(PS = \mathrm{expit}(\mathrm{cbind}(1, X)\,(1 + \lambda_2)\,\hat\alpha)\)); propensity score uses original \(n\) rows . augmented grid points raw penalty constant \(\lambda_n = (n + q)^{\delta}\) \(q = p\) augmentation rows (Balde's adaptive.lasso(lambda = n.q^(il)), n.q = n + q), penalty-scale constant uses \(1/(n + q)\) place \(1/n\), .e. \(s = \overline{\mathrm{pen}} (n + q)^{\delta} / (n + q)\). \(\lambda_2 = 0\) grid points run plain-OAL code path (augmentation, \(\lambda_n = n^{\delta}\)), method = "goal" lambda2 = 0 nests method = "oal" exactly. (Balde's script literally augments \(q\) zero rows \((n+q)^\delta\) base even \(\lambda_2 = 0\); zero rows still shift intercept, exact nesting deliberately preferred .) \(\lambda_2\) selected jointly \((\delta, \gamma)\) wAMD: flat first-minimum \(\lambda_2\)-outer \(\times\) \(\delta\)-inner grid, equivalent Balde's nested rule (per-\(\lambda_2\) minima \((\delta, \gamma)\), first minimum \(\lambda_2\)).

title: Outcome-adaptive lasso propensity scores — oal
text: Every grid point scored oal_wamd(): propensity scores clipped clip first (note Balde's reference implementation unclipped; see clip argument), IPW weights estimand, per-column weighted absolute mean differences standardized matrix summed weights \(|b_j|\) columns (including excluded ones). selected grid point FIRST minimum (relative tolerance 1e-9) grid order \(\lambda_2\) ascending (0 first) outer \(\times\) \(\delta\) ascending inner – ties therefore prefer plain OAL smallest penalty. wAMD balance heuristic: published proof guarantees lands \(\lambda_n\) inside window required theory (\(\lambda_n/\sqrt{n} \0\), \(\lambda_n n^{\gamma/2 - 1} \\infty\), \(\gamma > 1\)), inherits outcome model's misspecification vulnerability.

text: propensity scores first clipped clip; inverse-probability weights estimand (\(e_i\) clipped score) $$ATE:\; w_i = A_i/e_i + (1 - A_i)/(1 - e_i), \qquad       ATT:\; w_i = A_i + (1 - A_i)\, e_i/(1 - e_i).$$ covariate column \(j\) weighted absolute mean difference $$d_j = \left| \frac{\sum_i w_i X_{ij} A_i}{\sum_i w_i A_i}   - \frac{\sum_i w_i X_{ij} (1 - A_i)}{\sum_i w_i (1 - A_i)} \right|,$$ criterion \(\mathrm{wAMD} = \sum_j |\beta_j|\, d_j\), summed columns covs (including propensity score model excluded), \(\beta_j\) outcome-model coefficients supplied coef (absolute values taken internally). Inside oal(), covs standardized model matrix coef outcome coefficients standardized scale (wAMD, like adaptive weights, scale-dependent). formula implemented natively – never delegated cobalt – reproduces published criterion exactly.

title: oalasso 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-21 Initial release, implementing outcome-adaptive lasso Shortreed & Ertefaie (2017), Biometrics 73(4):1111–1122, doi:10.1111/biom.12679, generalized outcome-adaptive lasso (GOAL) Baldé, Yang & Lefebvre (2023), Biometrics 79(1):514–520, doi:10.1111/biom.13683. oal(): outcome-adaptive lasso propensity scores. Adaptive penalty weights |b_j|^-gamma unpenalized outcome model Y ~ + X full sample (gaussian() validated; binomial() allowed one-time warning, GLM theory per Baldé 2025, doi:10.1016/j.spl.2025.110379); tuning Shortreed & Ertefaie’s exponent grid lambda_n = n^delta paired penalty exponent gamma = 2*(gamma.factor - delta + 1); selection weighted absolute mean difference (wAMD) |b_j| weights, ATE (default) ATT weighting; ties take first minimum documented grid order. Exact reproduction published objective glmnet: every grid point evaluated s = mean(pen) * lambda_n / n coef(..., exact = TRUE), undoing glmnet’s internal sum--nvars penalty-factor rescaling per-observation loss scale (rejoinder recipe Jones, Ertefaie & Shortreed 2023, doi:10.1111/biom.13681). KKT conditions target objective acceptance criterion test suite, drift guard installed glmnet version. method = "goal": Baldé’s elastic-net generalization via Zou–Hastie data augmentation (1 + lambda2) rescale coefficients including intercept (Baldé 2025 supplement: expit(cbind(1, X) %*% (1 + lambda2) * coef)); augmented grid points (lambda2 > 0) raw penalty constant (n + q)^delta q = p augmentation rows, matching author’s adaptive.lasso(lambda = n.q^(il)) n.q = n + q. lambda2 selected jointly (delta, gamma) wAMD — flat first-minimum, equivalent Baldé’s nested per-lambda2---lambda2 rule — lambda2 = 0 exactly nests plain OAL (augmentation code path). default lambda2 grid c(0, 10^c(-2, -1.5, -1, -0.75, -0.5, -0.25, 0, 0.25, 0.5, 1)) author’s published grid (Baldé 2025 supplement, “taken Zou Hastie (2005)”), verified official GOAL code 2026-07-02. Baldé’s reference code runs UNCLIPPED IPW weights; default clip = c(0.01, 0.99) Shortreed–Ertefaie-lineage safety choice, clip = c(1e-12, 1 - 1e-12) effectively disables clipping reproduce reference behavior. Fixed standardization protocol: covariates standardized (training statistics, columns including dummies); outcome model glmnet fit matrix standardize = FALSE; coefficients returned scales. Options provenance labels printed every fit: refit = TRUE (post-selection unpenalized refit, Schnitzer et al. 2025, doi:10.1002/sim.70316, adapted longitudinal setting; convergence recorded per grid point), scalar gamma (Schnitzer-style fixed exponent crossed delta grid), outcome.coef hook (user-supplied weights standardized scale — screening use ; zeros become hard exclusions via glmnet’s Inf--exclude). Ecosystem integration: fit$ps (numeric, named rownames(data), clipped clip = c(0.01, 0.99)) drops MatchIt::matchit(distance = ), WeightIt::weightit(ps = ), psAve::psave(ps.append = ); oal_match() / oal_weight() wrappers reuse stored formula data; cobalt::bal.tab() works oal objects. Full S3 suite: print() (provenance line first; retained vs excluded covariates instrument-exclusion rationale Brookhart et al. 2006 Myers et al. 2011; literal next call; near-positivity diagnostic > 5% scores sit clip bound), summary(), plot() ("wamd", "coef", "balance"), coef() (scales), predict() (works without keep.fits), weights(). Exported criterion: oal_wamd() scores candidate propensity score exact S&E wAMD formula (single source truth; never delegated cobalt). Policies: NA used variables error naming variables (never silent drop); zero-variance aliased/rank-deficient covariates error; fully deterministic pipeline (seed argument); English-messages. Documented divergences author’s legacy fAL/fOAL code (superseded, reproduced): fAL gamma transcription bug, varlist wAMD bug, global-n scoping, per-subset scale(), archived lqa dependency. See vignette("method-details", package = "oalasso"). Three vignettes: Getting Started (matching weighting workflows, reading output), Using oalasso psAve (ps.append composition caveats), Method details provenance (formulas, exact penalty-scale correction, provenance table, relation software, honest nonlinear-extension notes).
